 137→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 138→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 139→                ),
 140→            ),
 141→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 142→            setOf(
 143→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 144→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 145→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 146→            ),
 147→    ) {
 148→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 149→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 150→
 151→    private var line = 1
 152→
 153→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 154→
 155→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 156→
 157→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 158→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 159→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 160→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 161→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 162→        )
 163→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 164→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 165→        }
 166→    }
 167→
 168→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 169→        node: ASTNode,
 170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 172→    ) {
 173→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 174→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 175→            node
 176→                .nextLeaf()
 177→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 178→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 179→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 180→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 181→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 182→                    }
 183→                }
 184→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 185→        }
 186→
 187→        when {
 188→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 189→                line++
 190→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 191→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 192→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 193→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 194→                    )
 195→                }
 196→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 197→            }
 198→
 199→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 200→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 201→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 202→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 203→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 204→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 205→                startIndentContext(
 206→                    fromAstNode = node,
 207→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 208→                )
 209→
 210→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 211→                visitValueArgument(node)
 212→
 213→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 214→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 217→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 218→                startIndentContext(node)
 219→
 220→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 221→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 222→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 223→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 224→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 225→                startIndentContext(node)
 226→
 227→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 230→                startIndentContext(
 231→                    fromAstNode = node,
 232→                    childIndent = "",
 233→                )
 234→
 235→            node.elementType == IF ->
 236→                visitIf(node)
 237→
 238→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 239→                visitLbrace(node)
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 242→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 243→                startIndentContext(
 244→                    fromAstNode = node,
 245→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 246→                )
 247→
 248→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 249→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 250→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 251→
 252→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 253→                visitValueParameter(node)
 254→
 255→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 256→                visitFun(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 259→                visitClass(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 262→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 265→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 268→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 269→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 270→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 271→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 272→                ) {
 273→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 274→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 275→                    //    .bar[0] {
 276→                    //        "foobar"
 277→                    //    }
 278→                    startIndentContext(
 279→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 280→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 281→                    )
 282→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 283→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 284→                } else {
 285→                    startIndentContext(node)
 286→                }
 287→            }
 288→
 289→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 290→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 291→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 292→
 293→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 294→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 295→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 298→                visitWhen(node)
 299→
 300→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 301→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 302→
 303→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 304→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 305→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 306→
 307→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 308→                visitKdoc(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 311→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 312→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 313→
 314→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 315→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 316→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 319→                visitLBracket(node)
 320→
 321→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 322→                visitNullableType(node)
 323→
 324→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 325→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 326→
 327→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 328→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 329→
 330→            else -> {
 331→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 332→            }
 333→        }
 334→    }
 335→
 336→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 337→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 338→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 339→            //     val foo = foo(
 340→            //         parameterName =
 341→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 342→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 343→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 344→            //     )
 345→            startIndentContext(
 346→                fromAstNode = node,
 347→                lastChildIndent = "",
 348→            )
 349→        }
 350→    }
 351→
 352→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 353→        node
 354→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 355→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 356→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 357→                val nextToAstNode =
 358→                    startIndentContext(
 359→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 360→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 361→
 362→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 363→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 364→                    startIndentContext(
 365→                        fromAstNode = node,
 366→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 367→                        childIndent = "",
 368→                    )
 369→                }
 370→            }
 371→    }
 372→
 373→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 374→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 375→        node
 376→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 377→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 378→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 379→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 380→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 381→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 382→            }
 383→
 384→        node
 385→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 386→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 387→            ?.nextLeaf()
 388→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 389→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 390→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 391→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 392→                    childIndent = "",
 393→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 394→            }
 395→        node
 396→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 397→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 398→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 399→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 400→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 401→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 403→            }
 404→        startIndentContext(
 405→            fromAstNode = node,
 406→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 407→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 408→        )
 409→    }
 410→
 411→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 412→        // Outer indent context
 413→        val rbrace =
 414→            requireNotNull(
 415→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 416→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 417→        startIndentContext(
 418→            fromAstNode = node,
 419→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 420→            firstChildIndent = "",
 421→            lastChildIndent = "",
 422→        )
 423→
 424→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 425→        node
 426→            .treeParent
 427→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 428→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 429→            ?.let { arrow ->
 430→                startIndentContext(
 431→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 432→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 433→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 434→                )
 435→                startIndentContext(
 436→                    fromAstNode = node,
 437→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 438→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 439→                )
 440→            }
 441→    }
 442→
 443→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 444→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 445→            // val fieldExample =
 446→            //      LongNameClass {
 447→            //              paramA,
 448→            //              paramB,
 449→            //              paramC ->
 450→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 451→            //      }
 452→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 453→        } else {
 454→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 455→            // val fieldExample =
 456→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 457→            //                     paramB,
 458→            //                     paramC ->
 459→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 460→            //     }
 461→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 462→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 463→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 464→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 465→                            " { "
 466→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 467→                }
 468→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 469→        }
 470→
 471→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 472→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 473→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 474→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 475→
 476→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 477→        startIndentContext(
 478→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 479→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 480→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 481→            childIndent = "",
 482→        )
 483→    }
 484→
 485→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 486→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 487→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 488→        node
 489→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 490→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 491→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 492→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 493→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 494→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 495→            }
 496→
 497→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 498→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 499→            //     fun process(
 500→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 501→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 502→            //     ): List<Output>
 503→            node
 504→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 505→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 506→                    nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 507→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 508→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 509→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 510→                }
 511→        }
 512→
 513→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 514→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 515→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 516→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 517→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 518→        ) {
 519→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 520→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 521→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 522→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 523→        } else {
 524→            startIndentContext(
 525→                fromAstNode = node,
 526→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 527→                childIndent = "",
 528→            )
 529→        }
 530→    }
 531→
 532→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 533→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 534→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 535→        val eqOrBlock =
 536→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 537→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 538→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 539→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 540→                fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 541→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 542→            ).prevCodeLeaf()
 543→        }
 544→
 545→        node
 546→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 547→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 548→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 549→                    fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 550→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 551→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 552→            }
 553→
 554→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 555→        startIndentContext(
 556→            fromAstNode = node,
 557→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 558→            childIndent = "",
 559→        )
 560→    }
 561→
 562→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 563→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 564→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 565→        node
 566→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 567→            ?.let { where ->
 568→                val typeConstraintList =
 569→                    requireNotNull(
 570→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 571→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 572→                require(
 573→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 574→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 575→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 576→                    fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 577→                    toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 578→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 579→            }
 580→        node
 581→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 582→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 583→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 584→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 585→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 586→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 587→            }
 588→
 589→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 590→        startIndentContext(
 591→            fromAstNode = node,
 592→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 593→            childIndent = "",
 594→        )
 595→    }
 596→
 597→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 598→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 599→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 600→        node
 601→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 602→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 603→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 604→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 605→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 606→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 607→            }
 608→
 609→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 610→        startIndentContext(
 611→            fromAstNode = node,
 612→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 613→            childIndent = "",
 614→        )
 615→    }
 616→
 617→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 618→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 619→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 620→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 621→            val conditionIndentContext =
 622→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 623→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 624→            startIndentContext(
 625→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 626→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 627→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 628→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 629→            )
 630→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 631→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 632→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 633→        ) {
 634→            startIndentContext(node)
 635→        }
 636→    }
 637→
 638→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 639→        startIndentContext(
 640→            fromAstNode = node,
 641→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 642→        )
 643→    }
 644→
 645→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 646→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 647→        node
 648→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 649→            ?.let { lpar ->
 650→                nextToASTNode = startIndentContext(
 651→                    fromAstNode = lpar,
 652→                    toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 653→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 654→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 655→            }
 656→        startIndentContext(
 657→            fromAstNode = node,
 658→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 659→        )
 660→    }
 661→
 662→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 663→        node
 664→            .findChildByType(ARROW)
 665→            ?.let { arrow ->
 666→                val outerIndent =
 667→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 668→                        ""
 669→                    } else {
 670→                        indentConfig.indent
 671→                    }
 672→                startIndentContext(
 673→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 674→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 675→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 676→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 677→                )
 678→                startIndentContext(
 679→                    fromAstNode = node,
 680→                    toAstNode = arrow,
 681→                    childIndent = "",
 682→                )
 683→            }
 684→    }
 685→
 686→    private fun visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node: ASTNode) {
 687→        startIndentContext(
 688→            fromAstNode = node,
 689→            toAstNode = node.nextCodeSibling()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()!!,
 690→            childIndent = TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 691→        )
 692→    }
 693→
 694→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {
 695→        node
 696→            .findChildByType(KDOC_START)
 697→            ?.nextLeaf()
 698→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 699→                startIndentContext(
 700→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 701→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 702→                    childIndent = KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 703→                )
 704→            }
 705→    }
 706→
 707→    private fun visitPropertyAccessor(node: ASTNode) {
 708→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 709→        node
 710→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 711→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 712→                nextToASTNode = startIndentContext(
 713→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 714→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 715→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 716→            }
 717→        // No indent on preceding annotations and comments
 718→        startIndentContext(
 719→            fromAstNode = node,
 720→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 721→            childIndent = "",
 722→        )
 723→    }
 724→
 725→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {
 726→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 727→        node
 728→            .findChildByType(BODY)
 729→            ?.takeIf { it.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType != LBRACE }
 730→            ?.let { rpar ->
 731→                startIndentContext(
 732→                    fromAstNode = rpar,
 733→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 734→                )
 735→            }
 736→        node
 737→            .findChildByType(CONDITION)
 738→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 739→                startIndentContext(fromAstNode)
 740→            }
 741→    }
 742→
 743→    private fun visitLBracket(node: ASTNode) {
 744→        node
 745→            .treeParent
 746→            .takeUnless {
 747→                // Should be resolved in IntelliJ IDEA default formatter:
 748→                // https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-14859/Too-little-indentation-inside-the-brackets-in-multiple-annotations-with-the-same-target
 749→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION
 750→            }?.findChildByType(RBRACKET)
 751→            ?.let { rbracket ->
 752→                startIndentContext(
 753→                    fromAstNode = node,
 754→                    toAstNode = rbracket,
 755→                    firstChildIndent = "",
 756→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 757→                )
 758→            }
 759→    }
 760→
 761→    private fun visitNullableType(node: ASTNode) {
 762→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 763→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 764→        node
 765→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 766→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 767→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 768→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 769→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 770→                    childIndent = "",
 771→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 772→            }
 773→        startIndentContext(
 774→            fromAstNode = node,
 775→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 776→        )
 777→    }
 778→
 779→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 780→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 781→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 782→        node
 783→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 784→            ?.let { eq ->
 785→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 786→                    fromAstNode = eq,
 787→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 788→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 789→            }
 790→
 791→        node
 792→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 793→            ?.let {
 794→                startIndentContext(
 795→                    fromAstNode = node,
 796→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 797→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 798→                )
 799→            }
 800→    }
 801→
 802→    private fun visitTryCatchFinally(node: ASTNode) {
 803→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 804→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 805→        node
 806→            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
 807→            ?.let { finally ->
 808→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 809→                    fromAstNode = finally,
 810→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 811→                    childIndent = "",
 812→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 813→            }
 814→
 815→        node
 816→            .findChildByType(CATCH)
 817→            ?.let { catch ->
 818→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 819→                    fromAstNode = catch,
 820→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 821→                    childIndent = "",
 822→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 823→            }
 824→
 825→        startIndentContext(
 826→            fromAstNode = node,
 827→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 828→            lastChildIndent = "",
 829→        )
 830→    }
 831→
 832→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 833→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 834→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 835→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 836→                modifierList
 837→                    .children()
 838→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 839→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 840→            }
 841→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 842→            ?: this
 843→    }
 844→
 845→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 846→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 847→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 848→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 849→        ) {
 850→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 851→        }
 852→        return fromAstNode!!
 853→    }
 854→
 855→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 856→        node
 857→            .parents()
 858→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 859→            .lastOrNull()
 860→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 861→
 862→    private fun currentIndent() =
 863→        indentContextStack
 864→            .peekLast()
 865→            .indent()
 866→
 867→    private fun startIndentContext(
 868→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 869→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 870→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 871→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 872→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 873→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 874→    ): IndentContext =
 875→        IndentContext(
 876→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 877→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 878→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 879→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 880→            childIndent = childIndent,
 881→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 882→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 883→            LOGGER.trace {
 884→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 885→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 886→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 887→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 888→            }
 889→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 890→        }
 891→
 892→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 893→        node: ASTNode,
 894→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 895→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 896→    ) {
 897→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 898→            LOGGER.trace {
 899→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 900→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 901→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 902→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 903→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 904→            }
 905→            indentContextStack
 906→                .removeLast()
 907→                .also {
 908→                    LOGGER.trace {
 909→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 910→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 911→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 912→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 913→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 914→                    }
 915→                }
 916→        }
 917→    }
 918→
 919→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 920→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 921→            indentContextStack
 922→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 923→        }
 924→    }
 925→
 926→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 927→        node: ASTNode,
 928→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 929→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 930→    ) {
 931→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 932→            return
 933→        }
 934→
 935→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 936→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 937→        val text = node.text
 938→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 939→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 940→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 941→                emit(
 942→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 943→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 944→                    true,
 945→                )
 946→            } else {
 947→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 948→                // been emitted.
 949→            }
 950→            LOGGER.trace {
 951→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 952→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 953→            }
 954→            if (autoCorrect) {
 955→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 956→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 957→                )
 958→            }
 959→        } else {
 960→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 961→        }
 962→    }
 963→
 964→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 965→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 966→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 967→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 968→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 969→        }
 970→
 971→        nextLeaf
 972→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
 973→            ?.let { comment ->
 974→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 975→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 976→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 977→                }
 978→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 979→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 980→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 981→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 982→                    return true
 983→                }
 984→            }
 985→        return false
 986→    }
 987→
 988→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
 989→
 990→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
 991→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 992→
 993→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
 994→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 995→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 996→        val adjustedChildIndent =
 997→            when {
 998→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
 999→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1000→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1001→
1002→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1003→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1004→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1005→
1006→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1007→            }
1008→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1009→    }
1010→
1011→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1012→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1013→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1014→            SPACE -> {
1015→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1016→                    emit(
1017→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1018→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1019→                        true,
1020→                    )
1021→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1022→                } else {
1023→                    nodeIndent
1024→                }
1025→            }
1026→
1027→            TAB -> {
1028→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1029→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1030→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1031→                    emit(
1032→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1033→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1034→                        true,
1035→                    )
1036→                    indentConfig
1037→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1038→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1039→                } else {
1040→                    nodeIndent
1041→                }
1042→            }
1043→        }
1044→    }
1045→
1046→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1047→        node: ASTNode,
1048→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1049→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1050→    ) {
1051→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1052→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(indentConfig)
1053→        }
1054→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1055→    }
1056→
1057→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1058→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1059→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1060→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1061→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1062→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1063→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1064→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1065→                }
1066→
1067→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1068→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1069→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1070→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1071→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1072→                }
1073→
1074→                else -> ""
1075→            }
1076→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1077→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1078→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1079→        } else {
1080→            ""
1081→        }
1082→    }
1083→
1084→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1085→        IndentContext(
1086→            fromASTNode = node,
1087→            nodeIndent = "",
1088→            firstChildIndent = "",
1089→            childIndent = "",
1090→            lastChildIndent = "",
1091→            activated = true,
1092→        )
1093→
1094→    private companion object {
1095→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1096→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1097→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1098→    }
1099→
1100→    private data class IndentContext(
1101→        /**
1102→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1103→         */
1104→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1105→        /**
1106→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1107→         * on which the indent context starts.
1108→         */
1109→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1110→        /**
1111→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1112→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1113→         */
1114→        val nodeIndent: String,
1115→        /**
1116→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1117→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1118→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1119→         */
1120→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1121→        /**
1122→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1123→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1124→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1125→         */
1126→        val childIndent: String,
1127→        /**
1128→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1129→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1130→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1131→         */
1132→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1133→        /**
1134→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1135→         */
1136→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1137→    ) {
1138→        val nodes: String
1139→            get() =
1140→                fromASTNode
1141→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1142→                    ?.leaves()
1143→                    .orEmpty()
1144→                    .takeWhile {
1145→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1146→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1147→                    }
1148→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1149→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1150→
1151→        fun indent() =
1152→            if (activated) {
1153→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1154→            } else {
1155→                nodeIndent
1156→            }
1157→
1158→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1159→    }
1160→}
1161→
1162→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1163→
1164→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1165→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1166→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1167→            Pair("[", "]")
1168→        } else {
1169→            Pair("", "")
1170→        }
1171→    return prefix
1172→        .plus(
1173→            this
1174→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1175→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1176→        ).plus(suffix)
1177→}
1178→
1179→private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1180→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1181→        expectedIndent: String,
1182→        node: ASTNode,
1183→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1184→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1185→    ) {
1186→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1187→        node
1188→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1189→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1190→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1191→            ?.let { _ ->
1192→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1193→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1194→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1195→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1196→                    emit(
1197→                        node.startOffset,
1198→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1199→                        false,
1200→                    )
1201→                    return
1202→                }
1203→
1204→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1205→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1206→                    if (node.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n") {
1207→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1208→                        // should have no indent as well.
1209→                        ""
1210→                    } else {
1211→                        expectedIndent
1212→                    }
1213→                node.children()
1214→                    .forEach {
1215→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1216→                            (
1217→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1218→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1219→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1220→                                )
1221→                        ) {
1222→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1223→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1224→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1225→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1226→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1227→                                } else {
1228→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1229→                                }
1230→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1231→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1232→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1233→                                emit(
1234→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1235→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1236→                                        "string",
1237→                                    true,
1238→                                )
1239→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1240→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1241→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1242→                                    )
1243→                                }
1244→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1245→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1246→                                // the closing quotes.
1247→                                emit(
1248→                                    it.startOffset,
1249→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1250→                                    true,
1251→                                )
1252→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1253→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1254→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1255→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1256→                                        )
1257→                                    } else {
1258→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1259→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1260→                                        )
1261→                                    }
1262→                                }
1263→                            }
1264→                        }
1265→                    }
1266→            }
1267→    }
1268→
1269→    /**
1270→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1271→     * the closing quotes.
1272→     */
1273→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1274→        children()
1275→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1276→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1277→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1278→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1279→            .let { indents ->
1280→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1281→                    indents
1282→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1283→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1284→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1285→                } else {
1286→                    indents
1287→                }
1288→            }
1289→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1290→            .minOrNull()
1291→            ?: 0
1292→
1293→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1294→
1295→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1296→
1297→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1298→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1299→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1300→
1301→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1302→        for (child in node.children()) {
1303→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1304→                val v = child.text
1305→                if (v == "\n") {
1306→                    return true
1307→                }
1308→            }
1309→        }
1310→        return false
1311→    }
1312→
1313→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1314→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1315→        val nonBlankLines =
1316→            this
1317→                .text
1318→                .split("\n")
1319→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1320→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1321→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1322→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1323→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1324→            nonBlankLines
1325→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1326→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1327→                }
1328→                .toCharArray()
1329→                .distinct()
1330→                .count()
1331→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1332→    }
1333→
1334→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1335→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1336→
1337→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1338→
1339→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1340→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1341→
1342→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1343→
1344→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1345→
1346→    /**
1347→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1348→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1349→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1350→     * split index.
1351→     */
1352→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1353→        assert(index >= 0)
1354→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1355→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1356→                if (it == -1) {
1357→                    this.length
1358→                } else {
1359→                    it
1360→                }
1361→            }
1362→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1363→        return Pair(
1364→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1365→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1366→        )
1367→    }
1368→
1369→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1370→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1371→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1372→            this
1373→        } else {
1374→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1375→        }
1376→    }
1377→
1378→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1379→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1380→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1381→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1382→        }
1383→        return node != this
1384→    }
1385→
1386→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1387→}
1388→
1389→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId
1390→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.findCompositeParentElementOfType
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 13→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 14→
 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}
107→
108→private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId
115→

 77→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 78→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 79→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression
 80→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtSuperTypeList
